The current best in the archive is 0.9409 (Program 3/4), which uses self-limiting logistic autocatalysis `c*(P-c)` plus a Langmuir-occupancy inhibitor. The parent here (0.9394) uses a saturable Hill inhibitor with linear elongation. The shape loss (~0.096) dominates the loss versus nmse (~0.054), so improving kinetic shape is the highest-leverage move.

Let me look at the winning direction (logistic autocatalysis got shape from 0.096 → 0.088). I'll build on that but target the shape further. Let me check the run history summary briefly to avoid repeating tried structures.
Empty. Let me check the dataset shape quickly to understand the curves.

Interesting — M0 and m0 are constant here (M0=1e-6, m0=3e-6), only cd varies. So x1 and x2 are effectively constant across curves, and cd (x3) is the discriminating feature. This means the seed/monomer channels differ only through fitted constants; the whole variation between curves is driven by cd's effect on timing. Good to know — the shape loss is entirely about how cd delays onset/half-time.

The winning Program 3/4 (0.9409) combines a nucleation floor + logistic autocatalysis `c*(P-c)`, with Langmuir occupancy inhibitor. The key improvement over the parent was the self-limiting logistic term for burst shape.

My strategy: combine the best of both — logistic autocatalysis (for burst shape) with the **saturable Hill inhibitor** (which the parent uses) but applied to the occupancy properly, and importantly let the inhibitor delay the **onset** more sharply. Since all curves share m0/M0, the extra channels are somewhat redundant. Let me create a refined version building on Program 3 with an added Hill cooperativity in the occupancy to better bend the timing curve.

- m0-dependence (monomer concentration determines rate & half-time),
